flashcards.q: What is a tangent to a circle?
flashcards.a: A tangent to a circle is a line that intersects the circle at exactly one point, called the point of contact.

flashcards.q: What is a secant of a circle?
flashcards.a: A secant of a circle is a line that intersects the circle at two distinct points.

flashcards.q: What is a non-intersecting line with respect to a circle?
flashcards.a: A non-intersecting line is a line that does not intersect the circle at any point.

flashcards.q: What is the point of contact?
flashcards.a: The point of contact is the unique point where a tangent touches the circle.

flashcards.q: How many tangents can a circle have?
flashcards.a: A circle has infinitely many tangents, one at each point on the circle.

flashcards.q: State Theorem 10.1 about tangents and radii.
flashcards.a: The tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius through the point of contact.

flashcards.q: At any point on a circle, how many tangents exist?
flashcards.a: At any point on a circle, there is exactly one tangent.

flashcards.q: Can two parallel lines both be tangent to the same circle?
flashcards.a: Yes, there can be at most two parallel tangents to a circle, on opposite sides.

flashcards.q: If a line is perpendicular to the radius at a point on the circle, what can you conclude?
flashcards.a: The line is a tangent to the circle at that point.

flashcards.q: How many tangents can be drawn from a point inside the circle?
flashcards.a: No tangent can be drawn from a point inside the circle.

flashcards.q: How many tangents can be drawn from a point on the circle?
flashcards.a: Exactly one tangent can be drawn from a point on the circle.

flashcards.q: How many tangents can be drawn from a point outside the circle?
flashcards.a: Exactly two tangents can be drawn from a point outside the circle.

flashcards.q: What is the length of a tangent from an external point?
flashcards.a: The length of a tangent from an external point to a circle is the distance from that point to the point of contact.

flashcards.q: State Theorem 10.2 about tangents from an external point.
flashcards.a: The lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.

flashcards.q: If two tangents are drawn from an external point P to a circle with center O, what relationship exists between OP and the angle between the tangents?
flashcards.a: OP bisects the angle between the two tangents; OP is the angle bisector.

flashcards.q: In a circle with center O, if tangents at points A and B meet at point P, what is the relationship between PA and PB?
flashcards.a: PA = PB (the tangent lengths from P are equal).

flashcards.q: If a quadrilateral is circumscribed about a circle, what relationship holds between its sides?
flashcards.a: The sum of opposite sides are equal: AB + CD = BC + AD.

flashcards.q: What is a tangent perpendicular to at the point of contact?
flashcards.a: A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.

flashcards.q: In what way is a tangent a special case of a secant?
flashcards.a: A tangent is a secant where the two points of intersection coincide at a single point.

flashcards.q: If O is the center and T is a point on the circle, and PT is a tangent at T, what angle is OTP?
flashcards.a: Angle OTP = 90°.

flashcards.q: How do you prove that a line is tangent to a circle from the center?
flashcards.a: Show that the line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of intersection with the circle.

flashcards.q: If two circles are tangent to each other, what relationship exists between their centers and radii?
flashcards.a: For external tangency: distance between centers = sum of radii. For internal tangency: distance between centers = difference of radii.

flashcards.q: What is the common chord of two intersecting circles?
flashcards.a: The common chord is the line segment joining the two points of intersection of the circles.

flashcards.q: If a tangent and a chord meet at a point on the circle, what can you say about the angle between them?
flashcards.a: The angle between the tangent and the chord equals the angle in the alternate segment.

flashcards.q: How many tangents parallel to a given secant can a circle have?
flashcards.a: A circle can have at most two tangents parallel to a given secant, one on each side.

flashcards.q: In the context of a pulley and rope, what is the relationship between the rope and the pulley?
flashcards.a: The rope is a tangent to the circular pulley at the points where it leaves the pulley.

flashcards.q: If PT and PS are tangents from external point P to a circle with center O, what can you say about the quadrilateral PTOS?
flashcards.a: PTOS is a kite with PT = PS, and the diagonals are perpendicular.

flashcards.q: In two concentric circles, if a chord of the larger circle is tangent to the smaller circle, how does it divide?
flashcards.a: It is bisected at the point of contact with the smaller circle.

flashcards.q: What is the locus of points from which equal tangents can be drawn to a circle?
flashcards.a: Any point on the line passing through the center; the lengths of tangents from different external points vary.

flashcards.q: If a polygon is circumscribed about a circle, what is the circle called?
flashcards.a: The circle is called the incircle or inscribed circle of the polygon.

flashcards.q: What property do all vertices of a tangential polygon share?
flashcards.a: All vertices lie on a tangent or at the intersection of two tangent lines to the incircle.

flashcards.q: Can a circle be drawn through the vertices of a tangential quadrilateral?
flashcards.a: Not necessarily; only special tangential quadrilaterals (like tangential trapezoids) may be cyclic.

flashcards.q: If tangents at the ends of a diameter of a circle are drawn, what can you conclude?
flashcards.a: The two tangents are parallel to each other.

flashcards.q: What is the angle of intersection between two tangents drawn from an external point?
flashcards.a: The angle between two tangents from external point P equals 180° minus the angle subtended by the chord joining the contact points at the center.

flashcards.q: In a right triangle, if a circle is inscribed touching all three sides, what is the relationship called?
flashcards.a: The circle is the incircle, and its center is the incenter of the triangle.

flashcards.q: If a tangent to a circle makes an angle of 30° with a chord drawn from the point of contact, what angle does the chord subtend in the alternate segment?
flashcards.a: The chord subtends an angle of 30° in the alternate segment (by the alternate segment theorem).

flashcards.q: How do you find the length of a tangent from an external point if you know the distance from the point to the center and the radius?
flashcards.a: Use the Pythagorean theorem: tangent length = √(distance² - radius²).

flashcards.q: What is the relationship between the angle subtended by a chord at the center and the angle made by a tangent with the chord?
flashcards.a: The angle made by the tangent with the chord equals half the angle subtended by the chord at the center.

flashcards.q: If a line is perpendicular to a tangent at the point of contact, what can you conclude about the line?
flashcards.a: The line passes through the center of the circle; it is along the radius or its extension.

flashcards.q: In a cyclic quadrilateral with an inscribed circle, what is true about the sum of opposite sides?
flashcards.a: The sum of opposite sides are equal (Pitot's theorem): AB + CD = BC + AD.

flashcards.q: What is the significance of the normal to a circle at a point?
flashcards.a: The normal is the line along the radius at the point of contact; it is perpendicular to the tangent.

flashcards.q: How many circles can pass through two given points?
flashcards.a: Infinitely many circles; their centers lie on the perpendicular bisector of the line segment joining the two points.

flashcards.q: How many circles can be tangent to a given line at a given point?
flashcards.a: Infinitely many circles; their centers lie on the perpendicular to the line at the given point.
